Article Title: Nominee vs legal heir: What is the difference and who has claim on assets? All you need to know
Article Text:
AI image

Who is a Nominee?



Who is a Legal Heir?

Difference between a Nominee and a Legal heir

Aspect Nominee Legal Heir Role Custodian Owner Transfer of Assets Can be done without any legal formalities. Requires testamentary documents for proof of ownership. Powers No power to transact, use, or transfer assets. Power to use and transact assets as an owner. Appointment Owner can appoint anyone, but some institutions specify certain conditions. Determined by succession/personal laws. Legal Right Cannot contest ownership legally. Can contest ownership in a court of law.

When individuals open bank accounts, invest in mutual funds, or buy insurance, they are often asked to name a nominee.However, many people mistakenly assume that the nominee automatically inherits the assets. In reality, the nominee is not always the final recipient — the ownership rights belong to the legal heir, reported ET.Understanding the distinction between these two is essential to ensure that your wealth is passed on to the intended beneficiaries.A nominee acts as a temporary custodian of the deceased person’s assets.The nominee’s role is limited to holding and managing the assets until they are legally transferred to the rightful heirs.According to ET, the nominee cannot sell, trade, or make any transactional decisions about the assets.The account or policy owner can appoint anyone — family, friends, or acquaintances — as a nominee, though financial institutions often prefer close family members.In some cases, the nominee and the legal heir can be the same person, which helps in ensuring a smoother transfer of assets.The legal heir or beneficiary is the rightful owner of the assets once the original holder passes away.To claim ownership, the legal heir must produce valid documents such as a will or other testamentary proof.If there is no will — a situation known as dying intestate — the heir is determined as per the country’s succession or personal laws, reported ET.While a nominee can initially access the assets, only the legal heir can claim them permanently or challenge ownership in court.Experts quoted by ET emphasise that having clarity on this difference helps avoid disputes and ensures that the savings reach the intended family members or beneficiaries.Therefore, it is advisable to regularly update both nominees and wills to align with one’s estate planning goals.
Article Summary: A nominee acts as a temporary custodian of the deceased person’s assets. The legal heir or beneficiary is the rightful owner of the assets once the original holder passes away. To claim ownership, the legal heir must produce valid documents such as a will.

Article Title: Life Certificate 2025: Step-by-step guide to generate and submit Jeevan Pramaan online and offline
Article Text:
What is a Life Certificate and why does it matter



Annual submission deadline for pensioners in 2025



1 November and 30 November.

How to generate Jeevan Pramaan online: Step-by-step process



Step 1: Biometric device registration

Step 2: Authentication and certificate generation

Electronic delivery and access of Digital Life Certificate



How can you submit a Life Certificate in 2025



Offline submission at a bank branch or CSC centre

Online submission via Jeevan Pramaan Portal

Submission through the UMANG App

Who needs to submit the Life Certificate



Central and State Government pensioners

Defence pensioners

Family pensioners in the event of the pensioner’s death

Pensioners from public sector undertakings

Key details required before submitting the Life Certificate



Aadhaar Number

Registered Mobile Number linked with Aadhaar

Pension Payment Order (PPO) Number

Bank Account Number where the pension is credited

Name of the Pension Sanctioning Authority

Is Aadhaar mandatory for a digital Life Certificate



Legal validity of Jeevan Pramaan



How Jeevan Pramaan simplifies pensioner verification



Every year, lakhs of pensioners across India must prove that they are still alive to continue receiving their pension. This verification, known as the Life Certificate or Digital Life Certificate (DLC), is a crucial and mandatory step in ensuring that pension payments are made only to eligible recipients, preventing fraud and misuse.Traditionally, this process involved visiting the bank or pension office in person, standing in long queues, and submitting a signed paper certificate. However, with the government’s focus on digitisation and technological advancement, this process has become much simpler, faster, and more convenient. Pensioners can now submit their certificates digitally from the comfort of their homes using Jeevan Pramaan, a secure, user-friendly, and Aadhaar-based platform.A Life Certificate is an official document that confirms a pensioner is alive. Without it, pension payments are automatically paused by the pension disbursing agency. This verification is essential for preventing fraudulent claims and ensuring that pensions reach the rightful beneficiaries.The Digital Life Certificate (DLC), also known as Jeevan Pramaan, is an advanced biometric-enabled version of the traditional paper certificate.Introduced by the Government of India, it uses Aadhaar-based biometric authentication using either fingerprint or iris scan to verify a pensioner’s identity. Once verified, a digital certificate is automatically generated and shared with authorised pension disbursing agencies.This online process eliminates the need for physical verification, providing elderly pensioners with a simpler and more accessible way to maintain their pension benefits.According to PIB, The submission of the Life Certificate is mandatory for all pensioners who wish to continue receiving their pension without interruption. For the year 2025, the standard submission period is betweenTo make the process more comfortable for the elderly, the government allows pensioners aged 80 years and above to begin the submission process earlier, from 1 October 2025. This extended window gives senior citizens more time and flexibility to complete the procedure without facing last-minute difficulties.The Jeevan Pramaan process involves two main steps: registering the biometric device and generating the digital certificate.Before using the service, pensioners or operators must register their biometric device such as a fingerprint or iris scanner. During registration, the user provides their Aadhaar number and mobile number, after which an OTP (One-Time Password) is sent to their mobile. After entering the OTP, they must provide their name and email ID before scanning their fingerprint or iris.Once authentication is complete, the system confirms that device registration was successful, allowing the user to move to the next step.In this step, the pensioner enters personal details such as Aadhaar number, mobile number, name, PPO number, pension type, sanctioning authority, disbursing agency, and bank account details. The system also asks whether the pensioner has remarried or is re-employed.After entering all details, the pensioner scans their fingerprint or iris using the registered biometric device. Upon successful authentication, the Digital Life Certificate is generated, and an SMS acknowledgment with the Jeevan Pramaan Certificate ID is sent to the registered mobile number.This certificate is automatically stored in the Life Certificate Repository, accessible anytime to both pensioners and pension agencies.Once the Digital Life Certificate is created, it can be electronically delivered to the concerned Pension Disbursing Agency (PDA). PDAs can log in to the Jeevan Pramaan portal to view, verify, or download the certificate directly.Pensioners can also download a PDF copy of their certificate by visiting the Jeevan Pramaan website and entering their Pramaan ID or Aadhaar number. This ensures transparency and easy access for future reference.The government provides several options to submit the Life Certificate, allowing pensioners to choose the method that suits them best.Pensioners who prefer traditional methods can visit their bank branch or a Citizen Service Centre (CSC) to submit their Life Certificate physically. Trained staff at these centres assist pensioners in verifying their details and completing the process.For those unable to travel due to age or health issues, India Post Payments Bank (IPPB) offers a doorstep banking service. Pensioners can schedule an appointment, and an IPPB representative will visit their home to complete the submission using biometric authentication. This service is available for both IPPB and non-IPPB account holders.For pensioners comfortable with technology, the Jeevan Pramaan portal provides a completely online process. By visiting https://jeevanpramaan.gov.in, pensioners can submit their Digital Life Certificate using a computer, laptop, or smartphone equipped with a fingerprint or iris scanner.The system uses the Aadhaar platform to authenticate biometric details. Once verification is successful, a Digital Life Certificate is generated and stored in a secure online repository. Pension disbursing agencies can then directly access the certificate, removing the need for physical paperwork.The UMANG (Unified Mobile Application for New-age Governance) app offers another easy option. Pensioners can download the app from the Google Play Store, search for “Jeevan Pramaan,” and select “Generate Life Certificate.”After entering the necessary details such as Aadhaar number, PPO number, and type of pension, pensioners must authenticate themselves using biometric verification. The app then generates a Digital Life Certificate (DLC), which is automatically shared with the pension disbursing authority.All pensioners who receive government, defence, or private sector pensions through authorised disbursing agencies are required to submit their Life Certificate every year. This includes:Failure to submit the certificate within the stipulated period may result in temporary suspension of pension payments until verification is completed.To ensure a smooth submission process, pensioners should keep certain details and documents ready. These include:Having these details ready helps to avoid delays or errors during authentication, whether submitting online or offline.Yes, an Aadhaar number or Virtual ID (VID) is mandatory to generate a Digital Life Certificate. The entire Jeevan Pramaan system is based on Aadhaar authentication, which ensures accuracy and prevents fraud. Pensioners must make sure their Aadhaar is linked with their mobile number and bank account before attempting to generate the certificate.The Digital Life Certificate is fully valid and legally recognised under the Information Technology Act. Pension disbursing authorities across India accept it as an official document, equivalent to the traditional paper-based life certificate.Its biometric verification system makes it secure and tamper-proof, reducing the chances of wrongful pension disbursal or misuse of pension funds.Before the introduction of Jeevan Pramaan, pensioners often faced difficulties in submitting their certificates, especially those with health problems or limited mobility. The digital system has removed these challenges, allowing pensioners to submit their verification anytime and anywhere.By integrating biometric technology and Aadhaar authentication, Jeevan Pramaan has streamlined pension management, improving efficiency for both pensioners and government departments. It also enhances transparency and ensures that genuine beneficiaries receive payments on time.
Article Summary: A Life Certificate is an official document that confirms a pensioner is alive. Without it, pension payments are automatically paused by the pension disbursing agency. This verification is essential for preventing fraudulent claims and ensuring that pensions reach the rightful beneficiaries. The Digital Life Certificate (DLC), also known as Jeevan Pramaan, is an advanced biometric- enabled version of the traditional paper certificate.

Article Title: What are gold, silver hallmarking charges? Check details and importance
Article Text:
What is gold, silver hallmarking?



Hallmarking charges



ALSO READ |

Testing for purity



The importance of hallmarking



Old hallmarking signs



ALSO READ |

Hallmark symbols



HUID in the hallmarked gold jewellery



Key components of hallmark on gold jewellery



Gold, silver hallmarking : Gold and silver jewllery have long been treasured for their beauty and value. However, ensuring their purity and authenticity is important, especially in a market prone to fraudulent practices. This is where the hallmarking scheme comes into play. Here’s what you need to know about hallmarking charges and its procedures:Hallmarking is the process of accurately determining and officially recording the proportionate content of precious metals in articles, such as gold and silver.It serves as a guarantee of their purity or fineness. In India, hallmarking is governed by stringent standards outlined in IS 15820, according to an ET report.Hallmarking charges are applied per article, regardless of its weight. For gold articles, the charge is Rs 45 per article, with a minimum charge of Rs 200 for a consignment. Similarly, for silver articles, the charge is Rs 35 per article, with a minimum charge of Rs 150 for a consignment. It's important to note that additional taxes and levies may apply.A consignment refers to a collection of articles with the same fineness, from which a sample is drawn and assayed for compliance. Different lots may exist within a consignment.Anyone can have their hallmarked jewellery tested for purity at an A&H centre by paying a testing fee of Rs. 200 (for testing using the fire assay method as per IS 1418). These centres must be recognized by the BIS. The list of recognized A&H centres is available on the BIS website under the hallmarking section.The primary objective of hallmarking is to safeguard consumers from counterfeit products. To achieve this, the Indian government made hallmarking mandatory for gold jewellery from June 16, 2021. Since April 1, 2023, jewellers are prohibited from selling any gold jewellery without a 6-digit Hallmark Unique Identification ( HUID ) number.If you bought gold jewellery on or before June 30, 2021, it may have old hallmarking signs. These signs include the purity/fineness mark and the BIS emblem, along with two additional indications: the jeweller's identification number and the assay centre's mark/number.However, starting July 1, 2021, these two indications were replaced with a unique alphanumeric HUID code. The hallmark remains valid for the lifetime of the jewellery, and consumers can sell old jewellery, whether hallmarked or un-hallmarked, to jewellers.The HUID, or hallmark Unique Identification number, is a key component of hallmarking in gold jewellery. It's a unique six-digit alphanumeric code assigned to each hallmarked item, allowing for traceability. Customers can verify the HUID number using the 'Verify HUID' feature in the BIS Care App.Since the introduction of the HUID on July 1, 2021, the hallmark on gold jewellery consists of three marks: the BIS logo, the purity of the article in caratage and fineness, and a unique six-digit alphanumeric HUID number.
Article Summary: Hallmarking is the process of accurately determining and officially recording the proportionate content of precious metals in articles, such as gold and silver. In India, hallmarking is governed by stringent standards outlined in IS 15820. Since April 1, 2023, jewellers are prohibited from selling any gold jewellery without a 6-digit Hallmark Unique Identification number.

6. Review and finalise

Points to keep in mind

Update your will regularly: You can keep your will up-to-date by regularly updating it on the platform to reflect any changes in your life or financial situation.

Communicate with family: It's also important to store your will safely and let your family know where to find it when needed.

Creating a will online: If you are planning to create a will, many online platforms offer easy-to-use tools and resources, allowing you to draft and finalise a will without leaving your home. Here's how to make an online will.Begin by choosing a reliable online service. Look for a platform that has a simple interface, comprehensive templates, and secure storage for your documents.To register, enter your personal information like your full name, date of birth, address, and contact details. Then create a secure username and password to access the online service.Next, make a list of all your assets. Include property, investments, bank accounts, insurance policies, and any valuable items you own.Decide who will inherit your assets by choosing your beneficiaries. You can assign specific items to each beneficiary and select backup beneficiaries in case your first choices can't inherit. Then, choose an executor, the person who will carry out your will.Use one of the customizable templates available on the platform. Follow the instructions to fill in the details, specifying how you want your assets distributed and including any special conditions or terms.You can review the drafted will to ensure it's accurate and make changes if needed. If you have complicated issues, it's best to get legal advice. Once you're satisfied, you can legally execute the will according to the platform's rules, usually by electronically signing it with witnesses. The completed will is then stored securely online, with encryption for added security.With inputs from Centre for Investment Education and Learning content which appeared in Economic Times
Article Summary: If you are planning to create a will, many online platforms offer easy-to-use tools and resources, allowing you to draft and finalise a will without leaving your home. To register, enter your personal information like your full name, date of birth, address, and contact details. Then create a secure username and password to access the online service.

Article Title: How to get your CIBIL credit score without a PAN card
Article Text:
Your credit score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity.

What is a CIBIL score?

How to get your CIBIL Credit score without a PAN Card

Factors affecting your CIBIL score

CIBIL credit score : Understanding your creditworthiness is pivotal in today's financial landscape, especially when seeking loans or credit cards. The Credit Information Bureau (India) Limited, commonly known as CIBIL, plays an important role in this regard by providing credit scores to borrowers.According to ET, credit score helps banks make informed decisions about extending credit cards and loans. Your credit history influences your CIBIL score , which is calculated using a special method.Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.1. Go to the official CIBIL website and click on 'Personal CIBIL score,' then 'Get Your Free CIBIL score.' Follow the link provided and fill in all the required details.2. If you don’t have a PAN Card, you can use an alternative identification such as Passport, Voter ID, Driver’s License, or Ration Card. Enter the number of the chosen ID.3. Provide your date of birth, PIN code, and select your state. Finally, enter your mobile number and click on ‘Accept and Continue’.4. Authenticate your identity by entering the OTP (One-Time Password) delivered to your mobile device on the next screen. Click 'Continue' to proceed.5. Choose whether you want to link the device with your account. Select 'Yes' or 'No' based on your preference.6. Upon successful registration, you will see a page confirming that 'You have successfully registered!'7. Click on ‘Go to Dashboard’ to access and view your CIBIL score.The factors influencing the new CIBIL score, as stated on the CIBIL website, include:1. Depth of credit: This refers to the duration of your existing credit history, calculated from the opening date of your oldest credit account.2. Long-term trend of outstanding balances: The overall trend of outstanding balances over time affects your score.3. Transaction history on credit cards: How you manage transactions on your credit cards impacts your score.4. Ratio of actual repayment amount to total amount due: This ratio indicates your repayment behavior and influences your score accordingly.5. Number of new accounts opened and number of accounts closed: Opening or closing accounts can affect your score, so managing these actions wisely is important.
Article Summary: Your CIBIL score is a three-digit number that reflects your creditworthiness and repayment capacity. Ranging from 300 to 900, a higher score indicates a stronger likelihood of loan approval. Banks and financial institutions use this score to assess the risk associated with lending money.

Article Title: AI is not built by software alone: Why the future needs interdisciplinary digital education
Article Text:
AI is not built by software alone: Why the future needs interdisciplinary digital education

.

Disclaimer - The above content is non-editorial, and TIL hereby disclaims any and all warranties, expressed or implied, relating to it, and does not guarantee, vouch for or necessarily endorse any of the content.

When young people think about the future of technology, the first words that often come to mind are Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning and Data Science. These are, without doubt, among the most transformative fields of our time. They are reshaping how we work, communicate, learn, travel, diagnose diseases, run businesses and respond to environmental challenges. Yet, the digital future is not built by software alone.Behind every intelligent system lies a broader ecosystem of knowledge. AI needs data scientists, but it also requires managers who understand both technology and markets. It depends on electronics engineers who design chips, sensors and embedded systems, and applied scientists who develop new materials and devices. It also calls for environmental experts who can use data to understand climate change, biodiversity, land use and sustainability. The future will belong not only to specialists in a single domain, but to those who can work across disciplines.This is the guiding philosophy behind the postgraduate programmes at Digital University Kerala . Established by the Government of Kerala , the University is dedicated to advanced education, research, innovation and the responsible development of digital technologies. Its academic framework is built around four interconnected themes: computing, intelligence, sustainability and entrepreneurship. Together, these themes reflect a simple yet powerful idea—the most meaningful progress happens at the intersections.For students planning postgraduate education, this shift is significant. A conventional degree may offer depth in one domain, but emerging careers increasingly demand the ability to connect multiple domains. A business analyst must understand data. A manager must grasp automation and digital platforms. An electronics engineer must work with intelligent systems. A physicist must engage with materials for future technologies. An environmental scientist must use digital tools, modelling and communication. Digital University Kerala’s programmes are designed with this evolving landscape in mind.One of the most dynamic areas shaping this landscape is Data Science and Product Development. Today, data is not merely analysed—it is transformed into products, platforms and solutions that impact millions of users. From recommendation engines and fintech applications to healthcare platforms and smart city solutions, data-driven products are central to innovation. Digital University Kerala’s programmes in this area equip students with both analytical and creative capabilities. Students learn to work with large datasets, build predictive models and translate insights into scalable digital products. Equally important, they are trained to understand user needs, design solutions, and take ideas from concept to deployment. This blend of data expertise and product thinking prepares graduates to contribute meaningfully to startups, technology firms and innovation-driven organisations.In addition, the University offers specialised programmes in Artificial Intelligence, Cyber Security and Data Science that address some of the most critical needs of the digital era. Artificial Intelligence programmes focus on building intelligent systems, machine learning models and automation tools that power modern applications. Cyber Security programmes prepare students to protect digital infrastructure, safeguard data and respond to evolving cyber threats in an increasingly connected world. Data Science programmes equip students with skills in data analysis, statistical modelling and decision-making, enabling them to extract meaningful insights from complex datasets. Together, these programmes ensure that students are well-prepared for core technology roles across industries.Management education is another area undergoing significant transformation. The MBA is no longer confined to traditional business administration. Organisations today are being reshaped by data, artificial intelligence, digital platforms, fintech systems, logistics technologies and automation. Decision-making is increasingly driven by analytics. Marketing is influenced by digital behaviour. Operations are optimised through technology. Finance is shaped by data systems and risk models. Human resource management is evolving with platforms, analytics and new modes of work.Digital University Kerala’s MBA programmes reflect this transition. With specialisations in Artificial Intelligence, Business Analytics, Marketing, Operations, Systems, and Human Resource Management, these programmes align management education with the realities of the digital economy. Students are encouraged to understand how technology transforms organisations, how data informs decisions and how innovation can be managed responsibly. For those seeking careers at the intersection of management and technology, this offers a forward-looking pathway.Another critical dimension of the digital world is electronics. While software often captures public attention, the physical infrastructure of digital technology is equally vital. AI today is embedded not just in computers and cloud platforms, but in chips, sensors, medical devices, robots, drones, smart vehicles, communication systems and industrial automation. The growth of semiconductor technologies, smart devices and connected systems has made electronics a strategically important field.Digital University Kerala addresses this through its programmes in Electronics Engineering, including VLSI Design and Embedded Systems. These areas are central to the hardware side of digital transformation. VLSI design focuses on creating integrated circuits and chips, while embedded systems bring intelligence into devices and machines. As India strengthens its focus on electronics manufacturing, semiconductor capabilities and smart technology ecosystems, postgraduate education in these fields becomes increasingly relevant.Applied Physics is another discipline gaining renewed importance in the digital age. Many emerging technologies are built on advances in materials, photonics, sensors, energy systems and device physics. From smartphone screens and solar cells to semiconductor chips and medical imaging devices, these innovations rely on scientific foundations that connect physics, materials and engineering.The M.Sc. Applied Physics programme at Digital University Kerala, with a specialisation in Applied Materials, is positioned within this critical space. It bridges fundamental scientific understanding with technological application. Students interested in materials science, electronic systems, sensors, energy devices and related areas can use this programme as a pathway between science and technology. In a world where innovation often begins with new materials and devices, this field remains highly relevant in the long term.The University also places strong emphasis on one of the most urgent knowledge domains of our time—the environment. Climate change, pollution, biodiversity loss, water stress, natural disasters, land-use change and sustainability challenges require more than traditional approaches. They demand data, modelling, satellite information, ecological informatics, visualisation, policy insight and effective communication.Digital University Kerala’s programmes in Environmental Science, Environmental Data Science, Ecological Informatics, Sustainability Studies and Environmental Communication reflect this evolving need. These programmes recognise that environmental challenges are increasingly data-rich and technologically complex. Students learn to combine ecological understanding with digital tools, equipping them to analyse problems and contribute to meaningful solutions. This is particularly relevant for a state like Kerala, where environmental resilience, disaster preparedness, biodiversity conservation and sustainable development are central priorities.The University’s broader portfolio includes programmes in Data Science and Product Development, Artificial Intelligence, Cyber Security, Data Analytics, Computational Modelling, BioAI, Geoinformatics, FinTech and related fields. What sets it apart is not just the range of programmes, but how they connect. Data Science links with product development. AI intersects with electronics. Geoinformatics informs environmental planning. Business analytics integrates with management. Applied materials contribute to future devices. This ecosystem approach defines Digital University Kerala’s distinctive character.Another key strength is its emphasis on practical, application-oriented learning. As the world of work evolves rapidly, postgraduate students need exposure to real-world problems, live projects, technology platforms and interdisciplinary thinking. Programmes that integrate classroom learning with project work, product development and industry-facing challenges help students understand how knowledge is applied beyond textbooks. This is especially important in digital fields, where tools, methods and expectations change quickly.For students today, the question is no longer simply, “Which course should I choose?” A more meaningful question is, “Which future do I want to prepare for?” Some may want to build intelligent systems. Others may want to develop data-driven products, manage technology-driven organisations, design chips and embedded devices, work with advanced materials or use data to address environmental challenges. Increasingly, many will seek to combine these interests.Digital University Kerala offers pathways for such aspirations. Its programmes point to a future where disciplines do not exist in isolation. The most impactful work will happen where fields converge—AI and electronics, data science and product innovation, management and digital transformation, materials and devices, environment and informatics.In this sense, Digital University Kerala represents more than a collection of postgraduate courses. It offers a model of education aligned with the realities of a rapidly changing world—one that recognises that the future will not be built by software alone, but by individuals who can connect technology with science, management, society and sustainability.See how Digital University Kerala is equipping learners with the interdisciplinary skills needed to thrive in a technology-driven future.
Article Summary: Digital University Kerala is dedicated to advanced education, research, innovation and the responsible development of digital technologies. Its academic framework is built around four interconnected themes: computing, intelligence, sustainability and entrepreneurship. The future will belong not only to specialists in a single domain, but to those who work across disciplines.

Article Title: Six homes per floor, no shared walls: Trifecta's new Sarjapur Road project bets on space over density
Article Text:
Six homes per floor, no shared walls: Trifecta's new Sarjapur Road project bets on space over density

Disclaimer - The above content is non-editorial, and TIL hereby disclaims any and all warranties, expressed or implied, relating to it, and does not guarantee, vouch for or necessarily endorse any of the content.

Trifecta Projects has unveiled Veranza, its latest residential development off Sarjapur Road in East Bengaluru. The project comprises 444 premium homes, across 2, 2.5, and 3-bedroom configurations, spread across two 36-storey towers.Taking a different approach to premium urban living, Trifecta Veranza dedicates 93% of its over 6 acre site to open, green, wellness and leisure spaces, creating one of the most spacious residential communities in one of Bengaluru’s fastest-growing corridors.Strategically located off Sarjapur Road, the project combines modern architecture with lifestyle-driven planning to create a more balanced urban living experience. By bringing together expansive open spaces, privacy, wellness and connectivity, Trifecta Veranza redefines modern urban living.The residences have been designed to address one of the most common concerns associated with high-rise developments: privacy and openness. With only six residences per floor, no two homes share a common wall or directly face one another, creating a greater sense of exclusivity and personal space. Complementing this planning are efficient layouts, generous ceiling heights, dual balconies in most residences and expansive 270-degree panoramic views from the living areas. Together, these features maximise natural light, cross-ventilation and openness throughout the homes.The project's defining feature is its extensive landscape planning. The vast 93% open expanse has been organised into distinct lifestyle zones that support recreation, wellness, work and community interaction. More than 75 curated amenities are integrated across the development to encourage active, social and mindful living.At the heart of the outdoor experience is a dedicated 1.3-acre multi-sport arena featuring a 23,087 sq. ft. mini stadium, along with pickleball, tennis and basketball courts, as well as a futsal turf. Separate social and community zones are designed to foster interaction and shared experiences, while solar-powered working pods offer residents the opportunity to work amidst nature. Wellness-focused spaces, meditation areas and a forest-inspired walking trail further contribute to a calm and restorative environment within the community.Further enhancing the lifestyle offering is a multi-level 28,900 sq. ft. clubhouse conceived as a self-contained vertical neighbourhood. The facility brings together indoor badminton and squash courts, a dedicated yoga studio, a unisex salon, an indoor multi-purpose hall and a landscaped outdoor event lawn for celebrations, gatherings and community activities. Residents also benefit from a library, guest rooms, hobby-oriented spaces, an on-site crèche and a ground-floor supermarket, creating a comprehensive ecosystem that supports modern urban lifestyles.Reflecting its commitment to sustainable living, Trifecta Veranza has earned Platinum pre-certification from the Indian Green Building Council (IGBC). The development integrates sustainable building practices, energy-efficient planning and advanced water conservation systems to create a more responsible and future-ready living environment.For Trifecta Projects , sustainability is not a recent shift but a founding philosophy that has shaped the company’s approach since its inception in 2012. Backed by over two decades of collective construction expertise, the developer has successfully delivered more than 4 million sq. ft. across 14 residential developments in East Bengaluru, reinforcing its commitment to creating thoughtfully planned communities designed for long-term living.With its emphasis on openness, privacy, wellness and sustainable living, Trifecta Veranza presents a distinctive residential offering for homebuyers seeking a more spacious and balanced lifestyle in East Bengaluru.Residences at Veranza are priced from ₹1.08 crore, with 2 and 3-bedroom configurations available. Visit their website to know more.*Terms and conditions apply.
Article Summary: Trifecta Projects has unveiled Veranza, its latest residential development off Sarjapur Road in East Bengaluru. The project comprises 444 premium homes, across 2, 2.5, and 3-bedroom configurations, spread across two 36-storey towers. With only six residences per floor, no two homes share a common wall.

Article Title: Apple iPhone 18 Pro data leak: Tata Electronics breach exposes key components — What we know
Article Text:
What has leaked:

An alleged ransomware attack on Tata Electronics has left Apple’s sensitive supply chain data exposed on the dark web. The leaked files reportedly include detailed lists of components and suppliers, along with photographs of Apple’s upcoming iPhone 18 Pro models. According to Reuters, the information is said to have been taken from Tata Electronics’ systems and later published online by the group behind the attack.The exposure could impact Apple’s tightly managed global supply chain, where hundreds of suppliers come together to build iPhones. It may also put pressure on Apple’s relationship with Tata, which has emerged as a key manufacturing partner outside China. The leak could additionally offer rivals, counterfeiters and vendors rare insight into Apple’s supplier network.Tata Electronics, which both supplies parts and assembles iPhones, is a key part of Apple’s push to expand manufacturing outside China. This move also fits into India’s plan, supported by Prime Minister Narendra Modi, to grow into a major electronics manufacturing hub.Apple is expected to launch its iPhone 18 Pro and iPhone 18 Pro Max in September. The leak comes as the company faces higher costs, after recently raising prices of iPads and MacBooks due to rising chip costs.Analysts expect iPhone prices may also increase in the coming months.Reuters reported that more than 200,000 files from Tata Electronics were posted on the dark web by a group called World Leaks. Those files reportedly included design documents for older iPhones and some Tesla parts, both linked to Tata clients. Documents linked to Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co (2330.TW) and Qualcomm (QCOM.O), both key Apple suppliers, were also part of the leak.New documents reviewed by Reuters show that at least six files map parts of the iPhone 18 Pro models to specific suppliers. These include parts for the main circuit board, battery and camera systems.A source familiar with the matter said Apple treats this supplier information as highly sensitive, especially for unreleased products. The documents reportedly show supplier details that Apple does not publish publicly.Overall, the files cover hundreds of parts expected to be used in the iPhone 18 Pro models. They also show where Apple uses multiple suppliers for some parts and fewer suppliers for others, revealing both strengths and risks in its supply chain.World Leaks has previously claimed responsibility for a breach involving Nike. Reuters has not confirmed the authenticity of the data and could not reach the group for comment. AppleInsider first reported that iPhone 18 Pro documents were included in the Tata leak.Reuters has reported that Apple is investigating the breach and working with Tata on security steps. Tata has restricted access to internal systems and hired a global consultancy for a forensic audit.Some leaked files also carry Apple “confidential” marks and internal code names linked to the iPhone 18 Pro series, according to a source.The leak folder also includes photos of iPhones being drop-tested at a Tata facility in early 2026. The images show a grey phone with a triple rear camera setup and the Apple logo. The source said these are iPhone 18 Pro models, although Reuters could not confirm this.India is expected to make 26% of global iPhone production in 2026, up from 6% four years ago, according to Counterpoint Research.
Article Summary: The leaked files reportedly include detailed lists of components and suppliers, along with photographs of Apple’s upcoming iPhone 18 Pro models. According to Reuters, the information is said to have been taken from Tata Electronics’ systems and later published online by the group behind the attack. The exposure could impact Apple's tightly managed global supply chain, where hundreds of suppliers come together to build iPhones.

Article Title: Researchers just found that job seekers may be better off replying fast, because an analysis of 11.6 million marketplace interactions suggests slower responses hurt hiring chances and quick replies ma
Article Text:
In today's job market, especially for freelancers, how quickly you respond to inquiries can significantly impact your chances of getting hired. Image Credit: ChatGPT

Speed Is a Signal: When

Faster Replies

Increase Hiring Likelihood

The hidden psychology of instant

digital communication

New research reveals that employers form quick impressions based on reply times, often interpreting faster responses as enthusiasm and reliability. This study highlights that even small delays can cost you opportunities, making prompt communication a crucial skill for professional success in the modern workplace. Image Credit: ChatGPT

Getting an undeniable advantage in the contemporary hybrid workplace

When we look at the small anxieties built into modern technology, few things are as tense as the text bubble. We watch those three little dots dance on our screens, or we glance at our phones, hoping for a notification banner to break a long, quiet afternoon silence. In personal life, people often read response times as a sign of anything from busyness to distance. For a long time, we treated these digital habits as simple, casual quirks of our social lives. But new tracking data shows that even small delays can matter when you are trying to land a job.Imagine submitting a spectacular portfolio or matching perfectly with a freelance listing online, only to have the entire opportunity slip away simply because you decided to wait until after dinner to reply to an initial inquiry. In the modern, always-connected freelance market, your professional destiny is often decided long before you ever jump on an introductory phone call or sit down for a formal video panel. A study found that employers make quick assumptions about a candidate's personality traits based on how long they take to reply.This profound behavioural pattern was detailed in a research paper published in the journal Management Science titled. The study utilised transaction data from approximately eleven point six million real-world marketplace interactions on a massive global freelancing portal.The data showed that longer reply delays were linked to a lower chance of getting the contract, making response speed an important competitive factor.To comprehend how the quick message influences contemporary recruitment managers, one should consider the psychological factors that exist beyond the screen. When the client contacts the freelancer or a potential worker, they do not only assess their technical competence, past projects, or star rating. What they need is to find the intangible level of professional security and trust between both parties. According to the results obtained during several experiment stages, a quick answer could be interpreted as a sign of enthusiasm and hard work.The behavioural trials suggest that replying quickly can change how an evaluator judges a candidate's character. Candidates who managed to reply within a tight window gave more positive impressions to the hiring managers than their slower counterparts. It also signalled future responsiveness, making employers more likely to see the candidate as organised and reliable.This study reveals the possibility that the halo effect could operate regardless of the message content or past quality metrics. Sometimes, even if the slow responder received better reviews or had a lower price, he still managed to win the contract. Reading a late message is likely to be taken as a signal of indifference or lack of organisation, which makes it harder to contact the slower employee under deadlines.The practical insights gained from this large-scale market research data provide the key tool for all those who are working in the competitive world of freelance and remote recruitment. In the world of hybrid workforces and teams around the globe, being reachable becomes a valuable professional skill that can be used to enhance one’s market value without any additional certification.By setting up notifications, keeping templates ready, and replying promptly during hiring windows, independent professionals can signal professionalism to potential buyers. This proactive approach helps build an immediate sense of social comfort and trust with a potential client before any physical work even begins. It can turn a digital exchange into a more personal interaction and give fast responders an advantage over slower competitors.The results of the research suggest that reaction time could be regarded as a signal. Fast reactions of the respondents will help to reassure the client, establish one’s reputation and increase one’s opportunities of being hired. Workers may demonstrate their professionalism by fast reactions.
Article Summary: How quickly you respond to inquiries can significantly impact your chances of getting hired. A study found that employers make quick assumptions about a candidate's personality traits based on how long they take to reply. Candidates who managed to reply within a tight window gave more positive impressions to the hiring managers than their slower counterparts.

Article Title: Microsoft has a new way to keep bots out of Teams meetings: Here’s how it will work
Article Text:
How Microsoft Teams’ new bot detection works



When detected, require approval before joining (default): Teams detects bots, places them in the meeting lobby, and requires organisers to approve them before admission.

Do not detect bots: Disables bot detection.

"Suspected threats,"

"Waiting"

“Admitting a bot should be a deliberate decision, not something that happens by mistake,”

“When Teams recognises that marker, it can identify the bot as a known participant,”

Microsoft is introducing a new feature in Microsoft Teams designed to prevent unwanted AI bots from automatically joining meetings. The company has started rolling out a new Teams admin policy that detects external bots, places them in the meeting lobby, and requires organisers to explicitly approve them before they can join.In a blog post, Microsoft product marketing manager Meera Ajam wrote, “Bots have begun joining meetings that participants never intended them to attend. For example, after connecting a third-party service to a meeting, some users have found that its bot continues joining future meetings automatically.”According to Microsoft, the feature is intended to reduce security and privacy risks caused by unintended bot participation in meetings where sensitive information may be discussed.The new policy, Manage external bots and their access to meetings, is available through the Teams Admin Center and can be assigned to individual users or specific groups.Administrators can choose between two settings:Microsoft said Teams now uses behavioural and infrastructure signals to distinguish bots from human participants with greater accuracy. When bots are detected, they are directed to the meeting lobby and grouped underwhile verified participants and registered bots appear under thecategory.The company has also removed one-click admission for identified bots. Organisers will now receive confirmation prompts before admitting bots, and warnings will appear if they choose "Admit all" when bots are included.Ajam wrote.Microsoft has also announced a Teams Bot Identification Program for independent software vendors (ISVs). Under the programme, bot developers will be able to register their bots with Microsoft and include a self-identification marker in meeting join requests.Ajam said. The company said it is currently testing the registration process with a limited number of ISVs before making it more widely available.As the feature rolls out, Microsoft said it will begin retiring the CAPTCHA verification system currently used for bots. The company also said it is exploring additional controls, including allow lists for approved bots, organisation-wide policies to block external bots, and audit reports showing bot activity in Teams meetings.
Article Summary: Microsoft Teams is introducing a new feature to prevent unwanted AI bots from automatically joining meetings. The company has started rolling out a new Teams admin policy that detects external bots, places them in the meeting lobby, and requires organisers to explicitly approve them before they can join. Microsoft has also announced a Teams Bot Identification Program for independent software vendors (ISVs)

Article Title: Quote of the day by Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei: "Fear is one kind of motivator, but it's not enough: we need..."
Article Text:
FILE - Dario Amodei, CEO & Co-Founder of Anthropic, speaks on a panel at the convening of the International Network of AI Safety Institutes at the Golden Gate Club at the Presidio in San Francisco, Wednesday, Nov. 20, 2024. (AP Photo/Jeff Chiu, File)

Quote of the day by Dario Amodei

A man who deals in risk decided to sell hope instead

Dario Amodei argues that fear gets attention, but hope sustains effort

It's a principle that reaches far beyond AI

Why it should stay with you

Other famous quotes by Dario Amodei

"Intelligence may be very powerful, but it isn't magic fairy dust."

"AI is so powerful, such a glittering prize, that it is very difficult for human civilization to impose any restraints on it at all."

"AI isn't a substitute for specific human jobs but rather a general labor substitute for humans."

Dario Amodei spends most of his time warning people. As the CEO of Anthropic, he has cautioned that AI could wipe out half of all entry-level white-collar jobs, hand bioweapon recipes to bad actors, and one day slip beyond human control. So it surprises people to learn that his most influential essay isn't a list of dangers at all. It's a vision of a world where everything goes right—disease defeated, poverty lifted, lifespans doubled. He wrote it on purpose, because he had concluded that dread alone can't carry anyone through a hard fight. You need something to run toward, not just something to run from. That's the idea behind the line—"Fear is one kind of motivator, but it's not enough: we need hope as well.""Fear is one kind of motivator, but it's not enough: we need hope as well."Amodei is best known as one of AI's most prominent worriers. His company researches how to keep AI safe, and he talks openly about catastrophe. But in his 2024 essay "Machines of Loving Grace," he deliberately set the warnings aside to describe the upside. His reasoning was simple: if all anyone hears is what might go wrong, they have no reason to push for what could go right.There has to be a positive-sum outcome worth rallying behind—something, in his words, to fight for.The quote draws a sharp line between two kinds of motivation. Fear is loud and immediate—it makes people freeze, flee, or scramble. But it burns out fast, and it rarely builds anything. Hope is what keeps people working through years of slow, uncertain progress. Amodei's bet is that the people steering AI through its most dangerous stretch won't be sustained by anxiety alone. They'll need a destination beautiful enough to justify the climb.Strip out the technology and the insight is plainly human. Every movement built on fear alone tends to collapse once the panic fades. The causes that endure—public health campaigns, scientific missions, social reforms—give people a picture of something better and ask them to build it. Amodei is applying an old truth to a new problem: you can frighten people into paying attention, but you can only inspire them into action.It's easy to be the person who points out everything that could go wrong. It's harder, and more useful, to also show what's worth the effort. Amodei's line is a reminder that warnings and optimism aren't opposites—they work as a pair. Fear tells you the stakes. Hope tells you why they're worth facing.
Article Summary: Dario Amodei argues that fear gets attention, but hope sustains effort. He wrote it on purpose, because he had concluded that dread alone can't carry anyone through a hard fight. Every movement built on fear alone tends to collapse once the panic fades.

Article Title: In 2014, Amazon released Echo, bringing always-listening voice assistants into ordinary homes
Article Text:
Amazon Echo, the voice-controlled smart speaker introduced in 2014 that helped bring AI-powered assistants into everyday household life.Image Credits: Wikimedia Commons

Turning the home into AI's first classroom

From answering questions to running the smart home

Amazon's consumer-focused innovations extended beyond online shopping with the launch of the Echo, a smart speaker that popularised voice assistants in homes.Image Credits: Wikimedia Commons

A device that changed how people think about AI

It took years before talking to a machine became normal. There was already voice recognition software, yet it had its flaws and was not widely spread. People continued to use keyboards, touchscreens, or remote controls to interact with machines. However, things took an interesting turn when Amazon came up with the Echo, a cylindrical smart speaker equipped with its virtual assistant named Alexa.Not only did Amazon release another consumer product; it also brought the concept of artificial intelligence into one of the places most familiar to people: the home. People stopped tapping screens and typing commands, and started communicating with machines the way they do with friends: with words. Amazon opened up a new way for millions of consumers to experience AI. Now that smart speakers have become quite commonplace items, it is worth remembering that their introduction was made possible thanks to the Echo.Amazon launched the Echo at a time when voice assistants were still unfamiliar to many consumers. Although speech recognition technology had existed for years, it had rarely been presented in a simple device designed to sit quietly on a kitchen counter or bedside table.A 2024 review published in the Journal of Medical Internet Research states that Echo changed that perception by combining natural language processing with an always-available home speaker. Instead of requiring users to learn complicated commands, Alexa was designed to understand conversational language. Whether someone wanted to play a favourite song, check the news, create a shopping list or set a cooking timer, they only had to ask aloud.This shift was significant because it made the home the primary environment where people learned to interact with artificial intelligence. Unlike smartphones, which were largely personal devices, the Echo became a shared household assistant that anyone in the room could use. Families gradually grew comfortable speaking to technology in everyday situations, making voice interaction feel less like a futuristic experiment and more like another household routine.Researchers studying smart speakers have noted that devices like the Echo became part of daily domestic life and helped normalise conversational interfaces across age groups and households.Amazon's consumer-focused innovations extended beyond online shopping with the launch of the Echo, a smart speaker that popularised voice assistants in homes.Image Credits: Wikimedia CommonsOne reason behind the Echo's rapid popularity was its ability to do more than provide information. As smart home technology expanded, Alexa evolved into a central control system for connected devices.Amazon News notes that users could switch lights on and off, adjust thermostats, lock doors, control televisions and manage appliances without touching a single button. Voice gradually became another interface alongside keyboards, smartphones and remote controls. Instead of opening multiple applications, people could complete simple tasks with a spoken command.This convenience encouraged broader adoption of smart home technology. For many households, the Echo became the first connected device they purchased before adding smart bulbs, plugs, cameras and security systems. As researchers have observed, voice assistants became an important gateway into home automation because they reduced the complexity of interacting with multiple devices.The shared nature of the Echo also distinguished it from earlier personal technologies. Rather than belonging to one individual, it became part of the household itself. Children could ask homework questions, parents could create reminders, and guests could request music, making AI a communal experience rather than a private one.Although Amazon did not develop speech recognition technology or create the first voice-controlled assistant, it presented Echo as a useful product that fit neatly into everyday routines. The Echo showed that artificial intelligence does not have to stay inside computers or smartphones. Instead, it can sit on a shelf and help with everyday routines by responding to voice commands.Since then, smart speakers have become a widespread phenomenon and even spawned other similar products, which contributed to the development of the smart home market. Now researchers assess voice assistants based on their ability to serve the needs of families, the elderly population, and people with certain disabilities.This evolution is one of the Echo's main achievements. The company managed to turn something innovative into an everyday thing. By normalising conversations with machines, Amazon changed the way people perceive artificial intelligence, one conversation at a time. More than ten years after its launch, the Echo is still seen as a milestone in consumer technology because it taught millions of people how to talk to machines.
Article Summary: The Amazon Echo was a smart speaker equipped with a virtual assistant named Alexa. It was launched in 2014 and helped bring AI-powered assistants into everyday household life. The Echo showed that artificial intelligence does not have to stay inside computers or smartphones. Instead, it can help with everyday routines by responding to voice commands.

Article Title: In 1982, computer scientist Sophie Wilson created ARM instructions that powered efficient smartphone chips
Article Text:
Sophie Mary Wilson (born Roger Wilson; June 1957) is an English computer scientist, a co-designer of the instruction set for the ARM architecture. image credit: Wikipedia

A small group with a simple objective

Why low power mattered so much

Risc PC is a range of personal computers launched in 1994 by Acorn, replacing the Archimedes series. The machines use an ARM CPU, developed by Acorn spinoff company ARM, and were thereby not IBM PC-compatible. image credit: Wikipedia

From desktops to

mobile devices

The lasting design legacy

The history of technology is frequently written about in terms of major advancements and great leaps in speed. But the device in your hand, the smartphone, has been created based on an entirely different principle – one of restraint. Back in the early 1980s, a group of engineers in Cambridge, UK, made a decision to create a computer chip that wasn’t all about pure speed and power. It was all about efficiency.This chip turned out to be the ARM processor, whose co-creator was none other than Sophie Wilson herself.In 1979, computer scientist Sophie Wilson worked at Acorn Computers. In 1983, Wilson and her colleague Steve Furber were starting work on an ambitious new project: the Acorn RISC Machine, better known as ARM.The initial aim was certainly not to transform the world or even create a handheld computer for the future. As explained in a historical feature in Selwyn College, Cambridge , the researchers simply wanted to create a useful chip that could be used in desktop computers.There were real limitations that the team had to work around. Because they lacked the vast resources of larger international firms, the researchers kept the chip simple. In particular, Sophie Wilson helped develop a reduced instruction set, which let the processor perform essential tasks with a relatively small set of commands and lower hardware and power demands.In the 1980s, low power consumption was a sensible engineering choice for desktop computers. Over time, that idea became central to modern device design.With the miniaturization of devices and the use of batteries, there appeared challenges for microchips. Any powerful microchip that drained a battery within minutes or generated too much heat would be useless.It turned out that the initial decisions made by Wilson and Furber were absolutely applicable in this situation. Because the ARM processor was designed with energy and heat limits in mind, it did not require fans or large power supplies. The chip delivered solid performance while remaining highly efficient.It was not until ARM became known for its efficiency that the technology took off as the solution for mobile devices. A peer-reviewed review paper, which is indexed in PubMed , reveals that the use of ARM cores by Nokia in its handheld products disrupted the entire mobile phone industry.As the mobile phones started developing into what we now call smartphones, the devices had to perform complex operations such as displaying digital content, browsing the internet, and playing mobile games. At the same time, the phones needed to fit easily into a user’s pocket. According to the peer-reviewed article, the success of ARM-based cores lies in their ability to deliver high-throughput performance using very little electricity in a small form factor.That particular technological triumph quickly revolutionized the whole consumer electronics market. Designers no longer had to worry as much about large batteries.Now, the work that Sophie Wilson did in the 1980s is reflected in the lives of billions of people all around the world. Although users mostly think about the brand name of their phones and app performance, the underlying approach has remained the same.By optimizing for low power consumption, low heat, and a simple instruction set, Sophie Wilson and her colleagues developed a chip that moved from desktop computers to mobile devices.
Article Summary: In 1979, computer scientist Sophie Wilson worked at Acorn Computers. In 1983, Wilson and her colleague Steve Furber were starting work on an ambitious new project: the Acorn RISC Machine, better known as ARM. The chip delivered solid performance while remaining highly efficient. It was not until ARM became known for its efficiency that the technology took off as the solution for mobile devices.

Article Title: In 1941, Hedy Lamarr and George Antheil created the technology that later inspired Bluetooth
Article Text:
Hedy Lamarr ( November 9, 1914– January 19, 2000) was an Austrian and American actress and inventor. Image Credit: Wikimedia Commons

A wartime solution through piano music

Why the idea took decades to emerge

George Johann Carl Antheil (July 8, 1900 – February 12, 1959) was an American avant-garde composer, pianist, author, and inventor whose modernist musical compositions explored the sounds – musical, industrial, and mechanical – of the early 20th century. Image Credit: Wikimedia Commons

The connection to contemporary Bluetooth

When we consider the technologies used in today’s wireless world, it is common for us to think about lab researchers and computer engineers. What should be noted, though, is the fact that one of the most remarkable ideas underlying current wireless devices emerged in the minds of a Hollywood actress and an avant-garde composer. Although initially it was supposed to help torpedoes find their way in the Second World War, the technique laid the foundations of current short-range communications.The path from their invention to its use in mobile phone chips has been long and circuitous. Their invention went largely unnoticed for decades between the worlds of science and engineering.Our story takes place during the peak of the war, when radio-guided missiles were very prone to jamming by the enemy. If the enemy found the frequency used to guide the missile, it would not be very difficult to disrupt the guidance system and make the missile miss its target altogether. As a solution to this pressing issue, Lamarr and Antheil developed a secret communications system. The Lemelson-MIT program reveals that this communication system enabled the transmission of a signal from a radio transmitter to a receiver by constantly shifting the frequency in sync with each other.In order to make the synchronization perfect, the two men sought inspiration from an unconventional place. The inventors used a device resembling a perforated piano roll so the transmitter and receiver could change frequencies in sync. It thus gave rise to a unique engineering solution to prevent torpedo control from being jammed. The application was submitted on June 10, 1941, while the patent was awarded as Patent Number 2,292,387.Although original, the idea did not find practical applications for decades, a historical timeline detailed by the American Physical Society in APS News Many inventions are ahead of their time because the technology needed to implement them arrives years later. The idea of frequency hopping was a very progressive one; however, in the 1940s, there were no appropriate hardware devices available that could implement such an innovation. Researchers from the University of Illinois Coordinated Science Laboratory say the concept did not become widely used until the 1980s. In these decades, digital technology and electronics became ready to realize this invention without any mechanical devices.This helps explain why Lamarr and Antheil's work was not widely recognized for so long. However, the core idea remained relevant because it was effective and useful. According to the Lemelson-MIT website, frequency hopping was implemented much later by the US Navy for military communication purposes.As wireless systems advanced, frequency hopping became one of the many members of the spread-spectrum technology family. As explained on the UCLA Samueli School of Engineering website, this mechanism eventually became part of many wireless technologies. Today, the basic principle of frequency hopping helps secure communication in GPS, Wi‑Fi, and emerging Internet of Things (IoT) networks.One of the best-known applications of this principle today is Bluetooth, which connects our wireless headphones, watches, and speakers to cars and smartphones. Even though Hedy Lamarr and George Antheil never created a consumer microchip themselves, the idea behind their patent lives on in billions of devices. The study published in the peer-reviewed journal Sensors proves that Bluetooth uses the principles of frequency-hopping spread-spectrum communication to transfer the information in different frequencies and time intervals.This constant hopping ensures there are no collisions in the signals and stability of connections in case the local frequencies get crowded. A further study in academia regarding Bluetooth Low Energy reiterates the importance of switching between channels at a rapid pace to ensure reduced interference in modern devices. Hedy Lamarr and George Antheil developed a smart solution for a certain issue of their era, but in the process established the foundation of modern connectivity.
Article Summary: Hedy Lamarr and George Antheil invented frequency hopping in 1941. The idea was to help torpedoes find their way in the Second World War. Frequency hopping was not widely used until the 1980s. Today, the principle of frequency hopping helps secure communication in GPS, Wi‑Fi, and emerging Internet of Things (IoT) networks.
